skill.name: x-filter
skill.description: Score and filter X topic ideas with weighted criteria for trend strength, controversy, value, and audience relevance.
skill.summary: Ranks collected X content ideas and separates strong topics from weak options.
skill.author: kangarooking

security_audit.summary: Static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown fences, example text, and the word criteria. The skill does instruct the assistant to read a specific hidden Claude skill profile file for relevance scoring, so it has a limited local filesystem dependency but no evidence of command execution, network access, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Local Hidden Profile File Access
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to check a user profile at a hidden Claude skill path for relevance scoring. This is a limited local filesystem dependency, but no evidence shows network transmission, secret harvesting, or arbitrary file reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact hidden home directory path is present on line 69. The surrounding workflow uses it only to score topic relevance, so the risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are false positives. The flagged lines are Markdown code fences and example report blocks, not shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line-numbered source shows fenced Markdown examples, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. No command names or interpolation patterns are present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings at the frontmatter description and scoring table header are false positives. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or encryption behavior appears in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is the skill description and line 12 is a Markdown table header. Neither contains cryptographic functionality.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Home Directory Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a hidden path under the user's home directory for profile-based relevance scoring. This should be disclosed because profile files may contain personal preferences.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path is explicit and located in the scoring instructions. No broader file access pattern is present.

content.value_statement: Creators often collect more X topics than they can use. This skill scores each idea with weighted criteria and builds a clear creation priority list.
actual_capabilities: Scores topics across trend strength, controversy, value, and relevance.
Separates ideas into selected, pending, and rejected groups.
Recommends content formats such as short posts, threads, and replies.
Uses a 10-point scoring model with a default selection threshold of 7.
Can reference a local user profile for account positioning.
limitations: It does not collect topics from X by itself.
Trend and controversy scores depend on the material supplied by the user.
It does not verify facts or source credibility.
Profile-based relevance depends on the availability and quality of the local profile file.

use_cases.title: Prioritize a Creator Backlog
use_cases.description: Rank a list of possible X posts and choose the strongest topics for the next publishing session.

use_cases.title: Filter Research Notes
use_cases.description: Turn collected articles, links, and rough observations into a scored list of usable content angles.

use_cases.title: Align Topics With Positioning
use_cases.description: Compare candidate topics against an account profile before spending time on drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Score a Short List
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-filter to score these X topic ideas. Show selected, pending, and rejected topics with brief reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner evaluation of a small topic list

prompt_templates.title: Rank Research Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-filter on these collected notes. Prioritize topics with high value and clear discussion potential.

prompt_templates.title: Match My Positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-filter and consider my account positioning. Explain how relevance changed the final ranking.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Creation Queue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-filter to score these topics, recommend formats, and produce a prioritized creation queue for this week.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a weekly content workflow

output_examples.input: Three AI product launch topics and two general startup news items.
output_examples.output: A ranked report that selects the most valuable AI launch topic, keeps one startup item pending, and rejects low-value news.

output_examples.input: A mixed backlog of thread ideas, reply ideas, and short post ideas.
output_examples.output: A prioritized creation pool with scores, recommended formats, and concise reasons for each decision.

output_examples.input: Collected links about developer tools, creator business, and unrelated celebrity news.
output_examples.output: A filtered list that favors developer tool topics and removes items that do not match the account focus.

best_practices: Provide enough context for each topic, including source, audience, and why it may matter.
Review borderline topics manually before rejecting them.
Keep the user profile current so relevance scoring reflects the account strategy.
anti_patterns: Do not use the score as a fact-checking substitute.
Do not submit single-word topics without context.
Do not optimize only for controversy when audience value is weak.

faq.question: Does this skill collect content from X?
faq.answer: No. It evaluates topics or materials that the user provides.

faq.question: What score is needed for selection?
faq.answer: The default threshold is 7 out of 10 points.

faq.question: Can I change the scoring weights?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes custom weights through a local user profile file.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Will it verify whether a trend is real?
faq.answer: No. It scores the material supplied to it and does not perform independent trend verification.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: It may read a local Claude skill profile for relevance scoring. Users should avoid storing secrets there.
